# Design of the Trading Agent

This chapter translates the reinforcement learning framework introduced in
Chapter 4 into a concrete agent design for single-asset high-frequency trading.
Each section addresses one component of the MDP formulation: the action space,
state representation, reward function, value function, policy, discount factor,
and step size. The choices made here are not arbitrary — each is motivated by
the structure of the order book environment and the practical constraints of
the trading problem.
